name: Billy Higgins
abstract: Billy Higgins (October 11, 1936 – May 3, 2001) was an American jazz drummer. He played mainly free jazz and hard bop.

concerts.label: Ornette Coleman and His New Septet

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Drummond
about_quotes.evidence: I have a tune, I don’t know if you’re familiar with a record of mine called “The Essence” on DMP with Hank Jones and Billy Higgins. A trio recording.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Drummond interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: I was fortunate that I played with Billy right up to the end. We had been playing together in Cedar’s trio for a number of years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: We introduced ourselves, and we all became friends. ... I had him come and be on my Stick Up album. ... The band that was really there for most of time while I was there filming was Herbie, Billy Higgins, and ... Billy and I would go to dinner every evening.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Carl Allen
about_quotes.evidence: I was playing with James Williams and Ray Drummond... Higgins... had come over... I heard this stick on my cymbal... it went from here through the roof. He wasn't playing loud... he was smiling
about_quotes.doc_title: Carl Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Atlanta, Georgia, January 4, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Cedar Walton
about_quotes.evidence: the trio with me, Sam Jones and uh, Billy Higgins... In our group was uh, the late Bob Berg on tenor saxophone, the late Billy Higgins and uh, David Williams
about_quotes.doc_title: Cedar Walton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charles McPherson
about_quotes.evidence: Billy Higgins who is I think from California or Texas or something like that. But that was the rhythm section of a gig many years ago, in the late sixties.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles McPherson interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 9, 2024 [interview 2 of 2]
